title: thelocephala riparia
intro: Thelocephala riparia, commonly known as "Hog's Larder" or "Wild Oyster Mushroom," is a type of edible fungus belonging to the genus Thelocarpon. This plant has been used in traditional medicine, offering several health benefits, including its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve immune function, and aid in the treatment of various diseases. The therapeutic actions of Thelocephala riparia include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The bioactive constituents of this plant include polysaccharides, proteins, and other compounds that may contribute to its health benefits, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects.
side_effects: Thelocephala riparia can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, diarrhea, and dizziness, which can occur due to the plant's high toxicity. Overconsumption of the plant can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding. Additionally, improper preparation and dosage can result in allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and respiratory issues. In severe cases, misuse of thelocephala riparia can even lead to convulsions, seizures, and coma.
